title: viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum
intro: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum, commonly known as the Doublefile Viburnum, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its showy, lacy white flowers and red berries. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its numerous health benefits. The therapeutic actions of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the body. The bioactive constituents of the plant include viburnic acid, isoflavones, and lignans, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum has many benefits, such as its ability to promote overall health and wellness through its high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. This plant has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression by regulating mood and sleep patterns. Additionally, the plant's antibacterial and antiviral properties make it an effective treatment for wound healing, skin infections, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. By incorporating Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved physical and mental health, leading to a higher quality of life and increased overall well-being.
actions: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. Its antiviral and antifungal properties can also aid in the treatment of viral and fungal infections, improving the overall well-being of individuals and enabling them to lead healthier and more active lives. Furthermore, the plant's potential to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality can have a significant impact on mental health, allowing individuals to better cope with the demands of daily life and maintain a sense of balance and calm. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum, individuals can experience improved physical and mental health, leading to enhanced productivity, better relationships, and a greater overall quality of life.
constituents: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, phenolic acids like ferulic acid and sinapic acid, and triterpenoids like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, as well as glycosides like viburnin and isoviburnin. These compounds have been found to possess various pharmacological properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, improve cardiovascular health, and even inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and viruses. The medicinal constituents of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum can also have a positive impact on daily life by reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and even supporting the immune system to fight off illnesses. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of these compounds can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, contributing to a longer and healthier life.
parts: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and berries, which are used to treat various health issues. The leaves of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, including viburnin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. The flowers and berries of the plant are rich in essential oils, including linalool and β-sitosterol, which have been used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Additionally, the roots of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum contain glycosides and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
preparations: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, that have been traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum is often used to treat digestive issues and promote relaxation, while its tincture is commonly used to alleviate fever and reduce inflammation. Infusions of the plant are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, and to promote wound healing. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal blends to support immune function and reduce stress.
side_effects: Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's leaves or berries can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The plant's leaves and flowers also contain a compound called amygdalin, which can release cyanide when ingested, leading to cyanide poisoning in extreme cases. Improper use of Viburnum plicatum subsp. glabrum can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and worsen underlying health conditions.
